next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 15

The International Conference Center smelled like floor polish and new carpet, the kind of institutional freshness that meant someone important was expected. Anya took her seat in the East Gallery, Damian's borrowed press credential clipped to her lapel, and opened herself to the room. The borrowed badge had given her better sight lines, a separate entrance, and still no answer about what list he'd kept her off.

Four hundred minds hit her at once.

She'd done crowd reads before. The diplomatic reception had been bad. The market extraction in her first week had been worse. The summit was something else entirely. Delegates from both nations packed the Main Assembly Hall below, and their collective anxiety pressed behind her eyes like pool water. She gripped the arm of her chair until the wood grain marked her palm and let the noise come through.

*should never have agreed to this format, the Westalians will use it to...*

*my daughter's recital is at four, if this runs over I swear...*

*Voss is here, which means the hardliners are treating this as...*

*did I lock the car...*

*coordinates confirmed, south terrace, fourteen hundred...*

Anya caught the last thought like a hook in cloth. She held still and tried to isolate the thinker. Male. Controlled. Operational shorthand. Four hundred people stood between her and the source, and the signal dissolved back into the murk before she could pin a face to it.

South terrace. Fourteen hundred. Ninety minutes.

She scanned the assembly floor. The Ostanian delegation sat in curved rows on the west side, dark suits and...
open_hooks_to_carry: Anya disclosing mission-critical information to Loid — enough to activate him
Yor brings the Garden-conspiracy intelligence from Ch 10 — the family reckoning: the Garden sent her to kill people whose deaths served the conspiracy
Yor's choice: she refuses to operate as a Garden asset; she will fight for her family, on her own terms — a quiet, decisive turn
The family planning session: Loid's tactical chess vs. Yor's direct-action methodology, mediated by Anya's secret telepathic optimization
Anya optimizes the plan using family thoughts they didn't volunteer — the efficiency works, and the fact that it works makes her feel worse
Bond's precognitive episode — barking at a specific location on Loid's map, connecting to Ch 6 vision
Damian arriving at the Forger apartment — the reader knows he knows who Anya is, giving every interaction new weight
Loid's involuntary shift into father-evaluating-romantic-interest mode, readable by Anya

payoffs_to_land.description: Bond's precognitive warning from Ch 6 is reinforced and partially clarified — he barks at a specific location on the summit security map
Yor's doubt from Ch 6, confirmed in Ch 10, reaches its turn — she chooses against the Garden

prev_tail_excerpt:
...al.

He remembered the safe house too, in ugly detail. Antiseptic on the cut above his eyebrow. Her fingers cold from rinsing out a washcloth. The way she said sorry like she meant the rib and not everything else. The kiss. There was no point pretending he had forgotten the kiss.

Was any of it real?

The girl in the photo was. The woman in the basement had been too. When she said she should've moved faster, there had been no performance in it. He knew performance. He had been raised on it.

He had been trained from childhood to see attachment as exposure. That training was not helping him now. He lied for a living too. Not the same lies. Close enough to matter.

He did not forgive her. The word was too neat, and too early. He also was not walking away. The raid and the ambush had happened on the same night. Reinhardt was exposed. WISE still had a mole. If he cut the alliance here, all he would be doing was finishing somebody else's work.

He would keep working with her. He would keep what he knew. Those were separate decisions, and he made both.

At the next meeting he would not say her real name. He would listen for the places she stopped herself. He would note what she chose to tell him and what she could not. He was done being blind, even if he was still very far from understanding her.

He slid the photo back into the box and put the box on the shelf behind the unread histories. Then he crossed the room, shut the bedroom window, and stood with his hand on the latch while the rain tapped once or twice against the glass. The apartment was still cold. It was only quieter.
secondary_character_beats: Yor's choice should be quiet and matter-of-fact — she is not dramatic about her own pain, but something has broken
Bond's precognitive barking should connect to the earlier dark vision from Ch 6
state_deltas_required: the operation is in crisis; the Forger family must assemble; Yor must confront the Garden's corruption and make a choice about who she fights for

The Forger family enters the operation as a unit. Yor chooses to fight as Yor Forger, not as Thorn Princess — her relationship with the Garden breaks. The plan takes shape. Damian is inside the family circle carrying knowledge the reader can see.
